notes.resolve: Resolve % for official entries are REAL, taken from the swe-bench/experiments repo (results/results.json, resolved/300). Darwin entries are our own gold-graded runs with the official swebench Docker harness.
notes.cost: Cost per instance for official entries is ESTIMATED from each system's DISCLOSED model(s) + scaffold shape × public token pricing — not a figure those teams published; treat as order-of-magnitude. 'undisclosed' = no public model/cost. Darwin costs are MEASURED from actual OpenRouter spend.
notes.conformant: Darwin solver never sees gold FAIL_TO_PASS/PASS_TO_PASS during solving; gold used only for scoring.

entries.name: Darwin · single-traj
entries.note: 102/300 full-300, gold, conformant; cost = measured OpenRouter spend

entries.name: Darwin · Best-of-3 + judge
entries.note: 13/25 pilot (87% union capture); full-300 pending; cost = measured

entries.name: ExpeRepair-v1.0
entries.note: est: Claude-4-Sonnet $3/$15 + reasoning models, 2+ attempts × heavy context

entries.name: SWE-agent
entries.note: est: Claude-4-Sonnet $3/$15 × long multi-turn agentic trace (~hundreds of K tokens)

entries.name: SemAgent Multi-v1
entries.note: est: two frontier models, multi-agent fan-out

entries.name: EntroPO R2E
entries.note: est: cheap 30B OSS model; closest cost-thesis peer. (Note: this model scored 0-4% in OUR scaffold — it does not transfer; harness-specific.)

entries.name: Agentless MCTS-Refine
entries.note: est: small 7B model, low per-token cost
